user_story_groups.story: When I ask local Codex to run a ticket, the spec should explain that Codexter reads the ticket, selects compute, routes skills, and writes proof without depending on Symphony.

When Symphony runs Codex in a Codexter-installed workspace, the spec should make clear that Symphony owns polling/retries/workspaces while Codexter owns skill routing, QA/review, and ProofPacket output.
When work later moves to Linear or another shared board, the spec should define an adapter boundary rather than tying Codexter to filesystem tickets forever.
When auditing future tickets, the spec should provide conformance checks and follow-up ticket boundaries so agents cannot quietly implement a daemon, cloud runner, or parallel Ralph early.

summary: The new spec is a strong canonical ownership contract for BoardAdapter, WorkItem, ComputeSelector, CodexterRunEnvelope, ProofPacket, local conversational mode, serial Ralph, and future Symphony/shared-board execution. It directly preserves the user's desired distinction: Codex is the execution engine, Codexter is the installed skill/proof layer, and Symphony is a future scheduler/runner that can launch normal Codex.

rubric_sections.name: spec-contract
findings: The spec gives one coherent ownership model and prevents drift into building a local Symphony clone.
rubric_sections.next_action: Use this spec as the grounding document for TASK-0112 through TASK-0116.

rubric_sections.name: implementation-plan
findings: The follow-up ticket map names exactly where implementation should happen and keeps adapter/runtime work out of this spec ticket.
rubric_sections.next_action: Proceed to BoardAdapter and compute-selector tickets after this spec is committed.

rubric_sections.name: user-intent-satisfaction
findings: The spec repeats the exact intended integration path: Symphony launches normal Codex, the workspace has Codexter installed, the prompt/file includes an envelope, Codexter routes skills, and Codexter writes ProofPacket.
rubric_sections.next_action: Keep future tickets aligned to that sentence-level contract.

rubric_sections.name: evidence-quality
findings: Validation covers doc parity, harness invariants, source registry, feature registry, and ticket metadata; this is proportional for a docs/spec ticket.
rubric_sections.next_action: Link the command outputs from the ticket Evidence section.

rubric_sections.name: integration-readiness
findings: The new spec is linked from README, ARCHITECTURE, specs index, harness techniques, source registry, and feature registry.
rubric_sections.next_action: Commit the spec slice and use it as dependency evidence for later tickets.
